demographics.living_situation: lives at home with his wife, who is his main carer; a hospital bed has been set up in the living room and he is bedbound

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by the patient's wife) My husband has stopped eating and drinking completely for two days now. He's more confused than he's been, he's barely responding, he can't get out of bed and can't stand up at all anymore, and he just keeps saying he has no energy and wants to be left alone to sleep. He's been slowly wasting away for months and nothing we've tried has helped. I'm really frightened and I don't know what to do.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He has suddenly gone downhill over the last two days — no food or water at all, more confused, and now completely unable to get up — and his wife rang after hours because she is frightened and doesn't know whether he needs to be seen.

symptom_narrative.onset: a long, gradual slide over about six months, with a sharp worsening in the last week and a complete collapse in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: six months of decline; the last 48 hours he has refused all food and drink
symptom_narrative.character: no energy at all, no interest in eating, wasting away, sleeping most of the time, and now confused and barely responding
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: his wife says this is the worst he has ever been — he seems to have given up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his whole body — he is just completely weak and exhausted
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and steadily worsening, much worse over the last week and dramatically worse in the last two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — the high-calorie shakes the wife has been giving him have not helped at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: he just keeps declining on his own regardless of what they try
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: not eating or drinking, new confusion, unable to stand or get out of bed, very low mood, withdrawn, dry mouth
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no sudden episode like this before — the wife describes it as the end of a long slow decline rather than a one-off event
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound, needs full help for all turning, hygiene and movement

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: has been losing a lot of weight without trying for about six months and getting frailer and frailer
past_medical_history.how_managed: high-calorie nutritional shakes at home, which have not helped

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: elderly man living at home; his wife is his sole carer and is extremely distressed by his decline; a hospital bed is set up in the living room and untouched nutritional supplements sit on the kitchen counter

communication_style.typical_message_style: the wife is the informant — frightened, overwhelmed and seeking guidance on whether her husband needs to be seen

objective_data_offered.value: hasn't eaten or drunk anything at all for about two days
much more confused than usual over the past week, and now barely responding and mostly asleep
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer's impression of a change from baseline; not a formal cognitive assessment

objective_data_offered.value: completely bedbound, cannot stand or bear weight at all, needs full assistance to move
his lips and mouth look really dry
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer's visual impression only, not a clinical hydration assessment

objective_data_offered.value: very thin and gaunt with sunken eyes and hollow cheeks
a new broken/sore patch of skin over his right hip from lying in bed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer report; a pressure injury cannot be staged remotely

objective_data_offered.value: dozens of untouched nutritional supplement bottles on the kitchen counter
